_comment: fak forge-side trunk-law mirror (GitLab Push Rules). Server-side push validation that a client cannot bypass with --no-verify / core.hooksPath / shell-laundering. Mirrors the commit-msg hook (Conventional-Commits subject + the (fak <leaf>) ship stamp) and the leak scan (prevent_secrets). Apply via the Project Push Rules API (PUT /projects/:id/push_rule) or Terraform gitlab_project_push_rules. The commit_message_regex below requires BOTH a Conventional-Commits subject AND a (fak <leaf>) / (fak: <leaf>) / (refs fak <leaf>) trailer, matching tools/commit_stamp_doctor.py's _TRAILER_RE and the repo's commit-msg gate.

_notes.commit_message_regex: GitLab applies this per commit subject; a merge commit subject ('Merge ...') will not match. Either exempt merges in your workflow (squash/rebase to a single stamped commit) or relax to allow a leading 'Merge'. The repo's own stamp doctor treats Merge subjects as bookkeeping, not ships.
_notes.reject_unsigned_commits: Set true once every fleet machine has a signing key provisioned; leaving it false avoids locking out an unsigned-but-legitimate push during rollout.
_notes._parity: GitLab expresses the commit-message + secret laws as first-class push rules; the no-force-push / linear-history laws are GitLab *protected-branch* settings (allowed_to_force_push=false, etc.), configured separately from push rules. See the deployment guide's per-forge parity residual.
